questions.question_text: What activities did you usually do to relax after a long day at middle school? Do you prefer spending your free time alone or hanging out with friends? Have your hobbies changed since you started Grade 10?

chunks.text: To be honest, finding free time is hard for Grade 10 students.
Nowadays, our schedules are very busy with heavy homework loads.
However, it is still very important to unwind.
Usually, many of my classmates act like a couch potato on weekends.
They just watch videos online and sleep all day.
Personally, I prefer active recreation.
For example, I am a huge basketball enthusiast,
so I always participate in school matches.
When I play sports with my friends, we always have a blast.
Overall, staying active is a truly fascinating pastime.

questions.question_text: Describe a leisure activity you enjoy doing regularly.  What it is and what equipment is needed. Who you do it with. Explain why this is your ideal way to relax.

chunks.text: Well, my favorite pastime is playing badminton.
Normally, I play it every Saturday afternoon at the local sports center.
Actually, I rarely go alone,
so I always participate with my best friends.
To be honest, I used to be a couch potato before high school,
but now, I am a huge sports enthusiast.
Because high school is very stressful,
I always get some exercise to unwind.
In fact, running around the court is a fascinating form of recreation.
To sum up, we highly enjoy this activity,
and we always have a blast.

questions.question_text: What show on TV do you find most fascinating and why? Do you usually participate in any sports to unwind after school? Are you more of a couch potato or an active enthusiast on weekends?

chunks.text: Honestly speaking, I spend too much time on my phone.
These days, short videos are very addictive.
I subscribe to many funny channels because they are entertaining.
However, my parents think I should watch the news broadcast.
They say it is more informative than cartoons.
Sometimes, I agree with them.
Actually, watching a good documentary is a great way to unwind.
Although the commercials are annoying, the show itself is fascinating.
In conclusion, I try not to be glued to the screen all day.

questions.question_text: Describe a memorable movie or TV show.  What it is and what happens in it. Who your favorite character is. Explain why you think it is worth watching.

chunks.text: Well, the most memorable show for me is 'Planet Earth'.
Basically, it is a nature documentary that is broadcast on the BBC.
To be honest, it is much more fascinating than a normal movie.
I subscribe to their channel so I never miss an episode.
The images are so beautiful that I am always glued to the screen.
In fact, it often makes headlines around the world.
Even though it is educational, it is also highly entertaining and addictive.
All in all, it is the most informative show I have ever seen.

questions.question_text: What is the most entertaining cooking show you have ever watched? (Recycled vocab) Do you prefer homemade food or eating out at restaurants? Have you ever tried to cook a nutritious meal by yourself?

chunks.text: To be honest, I have always had a huge appetite.
Recently, I watched an informative documentary about sugar,
and I learned that it is very addictive.
However, I have a sweet tooth,
so it is hard for me to stop eating cakes.
Usually, I try to consume healthy ingredients like vegetables and fish.
Although fast food is tasty,
I know that homemade food is much more nutritious.
Actually, I have tried Japanese cuisine many times, and I love it.
Overall, cooking healthy food is not a piece of cake,
but it is worth it.

questions.question_text: Describe a memorable meal you have had.  Where you had it and who you were with. What specific dishes and ingredients you ate. Explain why this meal was so memorable for you.

chunks.text: Well, the most memorable meal I have ever had was
a dinner at my grandmother's house.
Normally, we visit her every Spring Festival.
Actually, she is an amazing cook, and her homemade dumplings are delicious.
To be honest, the ingredients she uses are always fresh from her garden.
Because the food was so nutritious and tasty,
I consumed three huge bowls!
In fact, I have such a big appetite when I am there.
To sum up, traditional Chinese cuisine is my favorite,
and eating with family is never a piece of cake to forget.
Also, learning about her recipes was very informative and the taste was truly addictive.

questions.question_text: How do you usually commute to school every day? If you consume too much junk food before a long trip, how do you feel? (Recycled) Is the public transport in your city usually reliable?

chunks.text: To be honest, my daily commute to school is quite long.
Usually, I take the subway because it is very convenient.
However, during rush hour, there are too many passengers.
If I wake up late tomorrow,
I will definitely miss the bus and be late for class.
Sometimes, my father drives me in his vehicle,
but the congestion is terrible.
Actually, we always try to leave early to beat the traffic.
In my opinion, taking the subway is more reliable than driving.
Also, it's safer because I don't consume fuel or pollute the air,
which is not very nutritious for the planet.

questions.question_text: Describe a long journey you have taken.  Where you went and how you traveled. What happened during the journey. Explain how you felt about the trip.

chunks.text: Well, last summer I went to Beijing by train.
Actually, it was a very long journey, taking over five hours.
To be honest, the train was very comfortable and spacious for every passenger.
However, we almost missed the boat because we arrived at the station late!
If I visit Beijing again,
I will definitely take the high-speed train to save time.
Fortunately, the train was reliable and we arrived on time.
Overall, despite the congestion near the station,
it was a convenient way to travel.
I also managed to consume a very nutritious meal on board.

questions.question_text: What is your dream holiday destination? Is public transport convenient in your city for tourists? (Recycled) Do you prefer to plan a strict itinerary or just relax?

chunks.text: To be honest, I love traveling.
Last year, I went to Yunnan, which is a popular destination.
While I was looking for accommodation online,
I found a small hotel off the beaten track.
However, the traffic congestion on the way there was bad.
While I was hiking up the mountain,
I saw the most breathtaking view of the clouds.
Actually, I prefer to travel light so I can explore easily.
In the end, I bought a beautiful souvenir for my mom.
It was convenient to carry home.

questions.question_text: Describe an unforgettable trip you have taken.  Where you went and who you went with. What you did there. Explain why it was unforgettable.

chunks.text: Well, my most unforgettable trip was to Japan.
Actually, I went there with my family two years ago.
To be honest, we didn't plan a strict itinerary.
While we were walking through Kyoto, we discovered a breathtaking old temple.
Fortunately, our accommodation was very convenient and close to the subway.
Also, I bought a traditional fan as a souvenir.
To sum up, we loved to explore the city,
even though we got lost once!
We barely avoided the traffic congestion on the way to the airport.

questions.question_text: What was your favorite extracurricular activity in middle school? Did you have time to explore new hobbies last year? (Recycled) Do you find the current school curriculum difficult or easy?

chunks.text: To be honest, I used to find school quite stressful.
In the past, I had to complete a huge assignment every week.
However, I was always very motivated to get a good qualification.
Although the curriculum was hard,
I would always concentrate in class to understand the lessons.
Also, I loved extracurricular activities like painting.
Actually, art class allowed me to explore my creativity.
Sometimes, I created breathtaking drawings.
Because I wanted to improve,
I would often hit the books until late at night.
In short, hard work pays off.

questions.question_text: Describe a favorite subject you studied at school.  What the subject was. Why you enjoyed it. How it has helped you.

chunks.text: Well, my favorite subject used to be Geography.
Actually, I loved learning about the world and different cultures.
To be honest, I would always concentrate fully when the teacher showed us maps.
Since the world is so big,
I wanted to explore every breathtaking landscape.
Moreover, I was motivated to memorize all the capital cities by heart.
In addition, the curriculum included interesting assignments about climate change.
Even though the exams were difficult,
getting a high qualification in Geography made me very proud.
To sum up, it was a fascinating subject.

questions.question_text: What profession would you like to have in the future? Do you need a high qualification to be a doctor? (Recycled) Are you an ambitious person?

chunks.text: To be honest, choosing a future profession is difficult.
In my opinion, being a doctor is a noble job,
but you have to study for many years.
Also, you must get a high qualification from a university.
If you are motivated and hardworking,
you can become a successful candidate for the job.
However, some people just want to climb the corporate ladder
and get a promotion quickly.
Personally, I am ambitious, but I also want to help people.
Ideally, I would like to work with a friendly colleague
who can help me learn the ropes.
Finally, I would never resign if I loved my job.

questions.question_text: Describe a job you would like to have.  What the job is. What qualifications you need. Why you want this job.

chunks.text: Well, I would love to be a software engineer.
Actually, this profession is very popular nowadays.
To be honest, you have to be very smart and patient to write code.
Because technology is changing fast,
you must obtain a high qualification in Computer Science.
Also, I am very motivated to create new apps.
In the future, I hope to work with a brilliant
colleague at a big tech company.
If I work hard, I might get a promotion and become a manager.
To sum up, I am very ambitious about this career path.

questions.question_text: What is something you want to buy that might cost an arm and a leg? If you got a promotion, what would you do with the extra money? (Recycled) Do you think it is important to make a budget?

chunks.text: To be honest, managing money is very important.
Nowadays, many young people struggle to make ends meet because
the cost of living is high.
Personally, I try to save part of my income every month.
If I had a higher salary,
I would invest in the stock market to make more money.
However, my daily expenses are quite high.
For example, rent in this city costs an arm and a leg.
If I were more ambitious,
I would ask for a promotion at work.
In my opinion, keeping a strict budget is the only
way to be profitable in life.

questions.question_text: Describe something expensive you saved up for.  What you bought. How long you saved for it. Why you wanted it.

chunks.text: Well, I once saved up for a new laptop.
Actually, it was very expensive and cost an arm and a leg.
To be honest, I needed it for my studies.
If I didn't have a budget,
I wouldn't have been able to afford it.
So, I saved 20% of my income for six months.
If I hadn't been so ambitious about my goals,
I might have spent the money on other expenses.
Fortunately, it was a profitable investment for my education.
To sum up, I was very proud when I finally bought it.

questions.question_text: Have you watched a tournament recently? Is buying sports equipment a big expense for you? (Recycled) Do you prefer to be a player or a spectator?

chunks.text: To be honest, I love sports.
Recently, I have been watching the World Cup tournament on TV.
Actually, the matches are very competitive.
I have been trying to keep in shape,
so I do a workout every morning.
However, I don't have enough stamina to run a marathon.
Sometimes, the referee makes bad decisions, which makes the spectators angry.
Although sports gear is a big expense,
I have budgeted for a new pair of running shoes.
Let's get the ball rolling and go for a run now!

questions.question_text: Describe a sports match you watched or played.  What the sport was. Who was playing. Why it was exciting.

chunks.text: Well, I recently watched a basketball match at our school.
Actually, our team was playing against a rival school.
To be honest, the game was extremely competitive until the very end.
The players have been training for months, so their stamina was incredible.
Also, the spectators were cheering loudly for every point.
Although the ticket was a small expense,
it was worth every penny of my budget.
In the end, our team won,
and we decided to get the ball rolling on a celebration party.
To sum up, it was an unforgettable workout for my voice!

questions.question_text: Do you have a sibling who is very competitive? Do you have enough stamina to argue with your friends? (Recycled) Is it easy for you to compromise in a conflict?

chunks.text: To be honest, getting along with people can be hard.
For example, I have a sibling who is very competitive.
We often have a conflict about who is the best at video games,
which needs a lot of stamina to argue about.
However, he is also very trustworthy.
He is the only person who understands me when I
need a shoulder to cry on.
Usually, we try to compromise to solve our problems.
Actually, I am not very sociable with strangers,
so I value my family.
Although we don't always see eye to eye, we are best friends.

questions.question_text: Describe a friend who is important to you.  Who they are. How you met. Why they are important to you.

chunks.text: Well, my most important friend is Li Ming.
Actually, he is just an acquaintance from my football club,
but we became close.
He is a sociable person who loves to make everyone laugh.
To be honest, he is extremely competitive in sports,
and his stamina is amazing.
Even when we have a conflict,
he is the one who always tries to compromise first.
Also, he is very trustworthy with my secrets.
To sum up, although we don't always see eye to eye on movies,
he is always a shoulder to cry on when I am sad.

questions.question_text: Have you ever been a volunteer in your community? Do you think it is easy for a sociable person to work in a team? (Recycled) What is a charity you know about?

chunks.text: To be honest, volunteering is very important.
Last month, a charity event was organized by our school.
Actually, we wanted to make a difference in our community.
Many students were encouraged to volunteer,
and a lot of money was collected for the poor.
Personally, I think it is a meaningful activity.
However, there was a small conflict about the schedule.
Fortunately, we managed to compromise and work together.
In my opinion, being sociable helps when you want to contribute to a team.
If everyone tries to lend a helping hand,
a huge positive impact can be made.

questions.question_text: Describe a time you helped someone.  Who you helped. What you did. How you felt about it.

chunks.text: Well, I once helped my neighbor organize a charity sale.
Actually, the event was held in our local community center.
To be honest, I wanted to contribute because I believe
it is a meaningful cause.
The clothes were donated by many families,
and they were sold at a low price.
At first, my neighbor and I had different ideas,
but we decided to compromise.
Because I am quite sociable, I enjoyed talking to the customers.
A significant impact was made because we raised a lot
of money to lend a helping hand to those in
need.
To sum up, I was proud to be a volunteer.

questions.question_text: What is a major milestone you want to reach? How can you contribute to your school's success? (Recycled) Have you ever faced a difficult obstacle?

chunks.text: To be honest, learning English was hard for me.
Before I came to this school,
I had never spoken to a foreigner.
Actually, I had to overcome many obstacles.
I had dedicated hours to studying vocabulary before I finally saw an improvement.
Eventually, my hard work paid off.
Also, my teacher's help had contributed greatly to my progress.
By the time I took the exam,
I had accomplished my goal of learning 500 words.
It was a big milestone when I passed with flying colors.
In my opinion, this achievement had a huge impact on
my confidence to fulfill my dreams.

questions.question_text: Describe a difficult task you succeeded in.  What the task was. Why it was difficult. How you succeeded.

chunks.text: Well, a difficult task I succeeded in was learning to swim.
Actually, I was terrified of water.
Before I started lessons, I had never entered a pool.
Although I was scared, I had dedicated my summer to overcome this fear.
To be honest, the biggest obstacle was holding my breath.
After I had accomplished swimming one lap,
I felt like I had fulfilled a big dream.
My coach's advice had contributed a lot to my success.
In the end, my efforts paid off and I passed
with flying colors in the final test.
This milestone had a lasting impact on me.

questions.question_text: What can we do to reduce pollution in our city? Have you overcome any bad habits to help the environment? (Recycled) Is recycling popular in your country?

chunks.text: To be honest, the earth is in danger.
Nowadays, pollution is a huge problem.
Actually, what we see is just the tip of the iceberg.
If we don't protect animal habitats, many species will disappear forever.
Next week, I am going to start a conservation project at school.
We plan to recycle all our plastic waste.
Although it will be hard to accomplish our goals,
we must try to overcome the difficulties.
Personally, I want to live a sustainable and eco-friendly life.
So, I have decided to go green.

questions.question_text: Describe a beautiful natural place you know.  Where it is. What it looks like. Why you like it.

chunks.text: Well, I want to talk about a forest park near my home.
Actually, it is a perfect habitat for birds.
To be honest, I am going to visit it again this weekend.
Because the air is so fresh and there is no pollution,
it is a very eco-friendly place.
However, maintaining this beauty requires conservation efforts.
We must overcome laziness and accomplish the task of keeping it clean.
I think more people will choose to go green if they see this place.
To sum up, it is a sustainable paradise.

questions.question_text: What is the most useful gadget you own? Is electric car technology sustainable enough yet? (Recycled) Do you like trying virtual reality games?

chunks.text: To be honest, technology changes very fast.
My old phone is completely outdated now.
Actually, it is too slow to run new apps.
I want to buy an innovative new gadget,
but I don't have enough money.
However, there has been a major breakthrough in battery life recently.
So, I plan to upgrade soon.
Also, modern phones are more eco-friendly and sustainable.
Using a smartphone is not rocket science,
so anyone can learn it quickly.
In my opinion, virtual assistants are very helpful.

questions.question_text: Describe a piece of technology you use daily.  What it is. How often you use it. Why it is important to you.

chunks.text: Well, I use my tablet every day.
Actually, it is an innovative device.
To be honest, my laptop was too heavy to carry,
but this tablet is light enough.
It allows me to enter a virtual classroom and study online,
which is very sustainable.
Also, I often use it to control other smart gadgets in my home.
Because the company is ahead of the curve,
they release a major upgrade every year.
Although my old tablet is outdated,
it is still eco-friendly to recycle it.
To sum up, it is a major technological breakthrough for students.

questions.question_text: What is the most important tradition in your family? Are virtual festivals fun, or boring? (Recycled) Why should we respect other cultures?

chunks.text: To be honest, China has a rich heritage.
Our country is very diverse, isn't it?
Actually, every region has its own customs.
It is important to show respect to elders,
which is a key part of our etiquette.
However, some traditions are changing.
For example, young people often send virtual red envelopes now.
That is quite innovative, isn't it?
We should try to broaden our horizons by learning about other cultures,
but we must preserve our own traditions too.
Remember, when in Rome, do as the Romans do.

questions.question_text: Describe a traditional festival.  When and where it happens. What people do. Why it is important.

chunks.text: Well, I love the Spring Festival.
Actually, it is the most important tradition in China.
To be honest, the food is amazing, isn't it?
My family always follows the custom of making dumplings together,
which is part of our cultural heritage.
Also, we have to follow strict etiquette when greeting elders.
Although modern life is diverse and fast,
we still respect these old ways.
Sometimes, we use innovative apps to send virtual greetings.
To sum up, this festival helps me broaden my horizons about my own culture.

questions.question_text: Do you prefer a hectic city life or a tranquil country life? Is your city diverse enough for you? (Recycled) What kind of infrastructure does a modern city need?

chunks.text: To be honest, living in a big city is exciting.
Shanghai is a truly cosmopolitan place with a rich heritage.
However, the lifestyle can be very hectic.
The pace of life is not as slow as in the countryside.
Also, the infrastructure is amazing, isn't it?
But sometimes, people feel isolated in the crowd.
Although the city is diverse,
it is not as tranquil as my hometown.
Actually, I love the hustle and bustle of the streets.
In my opinion, living here offers the best of both
worlds: modern convenience and history.

questions.question_text: Describe the city or town where you live.  Where it is located. What parts are interesting. Whether you enjoy living there.

chunks.text: Well, I live in Hangzhou.
Actually, it is a beautiful city near Shanghai.
To be honest, the pace of life here is quite fast,
but not as hectic as Beijing.
The West Lake area is very tranquil,
whereas the city center is full of hustle and bustle.
Also, the infrastructure is modern and convenient.
It is a cosmopolitan city that still preserves its cultural heritage.
Although some areas are crowded,
I never feel isolated because the people are diverse and friendly.
To sum up, living in Hangzhou is the best of both worlds.

questions.question_text: Do you prefer buying trendy clothes or vintage ones? Is shopping in a mall too hectic for you? (Recycled) What is your favorite clothing brand?

chunks.text: To be honest, I love fashion.
Yesterday, I went to the mall to browse some shops.
Actually, I wanted to buy a trendy new garment for a party.
Because I wanted to look perfect,
I had my hair done at a salon.
However, designer brands are not always affordable.
Sometimes, I prefer vintage clothes because they are unique.
Shopping can be hectic, but finding a tranquil cafe to
rest in helps me relax.
Tomorrow, I plan to dress to kill.
My friends and I will probably shop till we drop.

questions.question_text: Describe a favorite item of clothing.  What it looks like. Where you bought it. Why you like it.

chunks.text: Well, my favorite item is a black leather jacket.
Actually, it is a vintage piece I bought in London.
To be honest, it was quite affordable for such a cool garment.
I usually get it cleaned professionally to keep it looking new.
Also, it is very trendy and matches everything.
Even when my life is hectic,
wearing this jacket makes me feel calm and tranquil.
I love to wear it when I browse the city streets.
To sum up, it helps me dress to kill whenever I go out.

questions.question_text: Have you visited an art exhibition recently? Do you think vintage movies are boring? (Recycled) What movie genre do you like best?

chunks.text: To be honest, I am not an art expert.
Last week, I went to a gallery to see a new exhibition.
Actually, I saw a beautiful,
large, modern masterpiece.
The painting was so colorful that it inspired me to start drawing again.
Also, there was a live performer playing the violin.
His music completely captivated the audience.
Although the gallery was quite trendy,
I preferred the vintage posters in the gift shop.
In my opinion, art should make you feel something.
Hearing the applause was music to my ears.

questions.question_text: Describe an art exhibition or performance.  Where it was. What you saw. How it made you feel.

chunks.text: Well, I recently saw a masterpiece at the city museum.
Actually, it was a fantastic,
ancient, Chinese vase.
To be honest, the exhibition was crowded with people.
The history of the object captivated me because I love this genre of art.
Also, the guide gave an inspireing talk about it.
It wasn't a trendy modern show, but rather a classic vintage display.
In the end, a young performer played traditional music which stole the show.
To sum up, it was a memorable cultural experience.

questions.question_text: How long does it usually take you to recover from a cold? What helps you recover when you are sad? Does music inspire you? (Recycled) Do you take any preventive medicine like vitamins?

chunks.text: To be honest, health is wealth.
Last month, I felt a bit under the weather.
Actually, my symptoms included a headache and fever.
The doctor diagnosed me with the flu and said that I needed rest.
He also told me to drink plenty of water.
Personally, I believe in preventive measures like exercise to boost immunity.
My coach said that daily therapy would help me recover faster.
His words really inspired me to take better care of myself.
Now, I feel fit as a fiddle.
Also, reading about health captivated my interest.

questions.question_text: Describe a time you were sick and recovered.  What the illness was. What the doctor said. How you got better.

chunks.text: Well, I remember when I caught a bad cold last winter.
Actually, my main symptom was a terrible cough.
To be honest, I felt completely under the weather.
My mom said that I had weak immunity because I didn't eat enough vegetables.
The doctor diagnosed a throat infection.
He told me that I needed to take some medicine to recover fully.
After a week of rest and preventive care,
I was fit as a fiddle again.
The experience inspired me to exercise more.
Also, learning about the body captivated me.

questions.question_text: What do you predict life will be like in 2050? Will technology help us recover from climate change? (Recycled) Do you have a clear vision for your future?

chunks.text: To be honest, the future is mysterious.
Experts predict that AI will revolutionize our lives.
Actually, this change is inevitable.
In ten years, robots will be working in many factories,
which has huge potential.
However, we must bear in mind the consequences.
For example, some jobs might disappear.
We will be living in a very different world,
so preventive laws are needed.
If we prepare well, society will recover quickly from any shock.
In my vision, a better future is just around the corner.

questions.question_text: Describe your life 10 years from now.  Where you will be living. What you will be doing. How your life will be different.

chunks.text: Well, in 10 years, I think I will be living in a big city.
Actually, I will be working as an architect.
To be honest, I predict that technology will revolutionize how we build houses.
I will be designing preventive systems to protect homes from storms.
Also, I have a vision of a green city.
This change is inevitable, and it will have a positive consequence for the planet.
We must recover the nature we lost.
To sum up, exciting times are around the corner,
and I see great potential.

questions.question_text: How has technology changed societal relaxation? Do you think a sedentary lifestyle is inevitable for office workers? (Recycled) What activities do you pursue to feel invigorated?

chunks.text: It could be argued that modern life is too fast.
Nowadays, many people have a sedentary lifestyle.
Actually, they spend hours sitting in front of screens.
Some experts predict that this will have inevitable health consequences.
However, it is important to pursue hobbies that stimulate the mind and body.
For example, hiking can be very invigorateing.
Others might say that solitude is therapeutic for mental health.
Personally, I need to let my hair down on weekends.
It allows me to recharge my batteries for the week ahead.

questions.question_text: Discuss how technology has changed societal relaxation.  What traditional relaxation was like. How we relax now. Whether this change is positive or negative.

chunks.text: It is often said that technology has transformed leisure.
In the past, people used to enjoy active games.
To be honest, today's youth are more sedentary.
It could be argued that digital entertainment fails to invigorate
us like physical exercise does.
However, online games can stimulate cognitive skills.
While some predict a decline in social skills,
others find solitude in gaming therapeutic.
It seems inevitable that we will rely more on screens.
Nevertheless, we must find ways to recharge our batteries offline.
To sum up, balance is key.

questions.question_text: What are the dangers of media bias and manipulation? Do news channels pursue the truth or just profits? (Recycled) How can we verify information we find online?

chunks.text: It is undeniable that media influences us.
Unfortunately, some news outlets manipulate facts to gain views.
Actually, they often sensationalize stories.
It is far more difficult to find objective reporting today
than it used to be.
However, we must try to verify what we read.
Personally, I take with a grain of salt everything I see online.
Social media disseminates information much faster than traditional TV,
but it often contains more bias.
Some content is designed to stimulate anger rather than inform.
Therefore, critical thinking is essential.

questions.question_text: Discuss the dangers of media bias.  Why media outlets might be biased. How this affects public opinion. What we can do about it.

chunks.text: It is often observed that media companies pursue profits over truth.
Consequently, they may manipulate headlines to attract clicks.
To be honest, this practice sensationalizes trivial events.
This report is nowhere near as objective as it claims to be,
showing clear political bias.
Moreover, fake news is easy to disseminate via word of mouth online.
It is slightly less difficult to verify facts if you check multiple sources.
We should stimulate open discussions instead of echoing one view.
To sum up, skepticism is a necessary tool.

questions.question_text: Why is there a global epidemic of unhealthy eating? Do food companies manipulate us to buy processed food? (Recycled) What are your worst dietary habits?

chunks.text: It is alarming that obesity has become an epidemic.
Actually, many people blame the food industry.
Companies often manipulate advertising to sell processed snacks.
If they had not added so much sugar,
the public wouldn't have developed such a strong bias towards sweet foods.
However, we also have a responsibility.
Sometimes, we indulge in fast food too often.
If I had known about the effect on my metabolism,
I would have changed my dietary habits sooner.
Although culinary skills are fading, cooking at home is best.
Remember, you are what you eat.

questions.question_text: Discuss why unhealthy eating is increasing.  The role of convenience. The impact of advertising. The consequences for health.

chunks.text: It is clear that our food environment is toxic.
Actually, processed food is everywhere.
To be honest, the marketing is very persuasive.
If the government had regulated ads earlier,
this epidemic might not have spread so fast.
Also, people love to indulge in mouth-watering burgers.
If I hadn't learned culinary skills at school,
I would have relied on take-out, damaging my metabolism.
There is a clear bias towards convenience over health.
To sum up, we must be mindful of our dietary choices.

questions.question_text: How can cities solve gridlock sustainably? Do people indulge in buying cars they don't need? (Recycled) Is cycling a viable option in your city?

chunks.text: It is obvious that traffic is a nightmare.
Every morning, the roads are bumper to bumper.
Actually, this gridlock drives me up the wall.
By 2030, I hope the government will have subsidized cleaner alternatives like electric buses.
Also, cars emit dangerous fumes.
Personally, I think we should integrate more bike lanes.
If we don't act, we will have processed too much
pollution into the air by next year.
We need a viable plan.
To sum up, public transport must improve.

questions.question_text: Discuss how to solve city traffic problems.  What the main problems are. What solutions exist. What the future holds.

chunks.text: It is widely agreed that cities are too crowded.
Actually, the gridlock causes wasted time and stress.
To be honest, we indulge in driving too much.
By the end of the decade,
experts hope we will have integrated AI into traffic systems.
This viable solution could reduce waiting times.
Also, if we subsidize electric cars,
we will have emitted far less carbon by 2040.
An alternative is to promote walking.
To sum up, we must avoid bumper to bumper roads.

questions.question_text: What is the cultural impact of commercializing pristine destinations? Is camping a viable alternative to hotels? (Recycled) Do you like to immerse yourself in local culture?

chunks.text: It is sad that tourism ruins places.
Many villages, which used to be quiet, are now noisy.
Actually, they have been commercialized to sell cheap souvenirs.
My hometown, which was once pristine, is now full of trash.
However, travel does broaden the mind.
Personally, I try to find authentic experiences.
I prefer small guesthouses, which allow me to immerse myself in the culture.
Also, finding an alternative route leads to picturesque views.
The scenery is often out of this world.
But, I wouldn't want to live out of a suitcase forever.

questions.question_text: Discuss the impact of tourism on culture.  Benefits of tourism. Negative effects on culture. How to be a responsible tourist.

chunks.text: It is undeniable that tourism brings money.
However, mass tourism, which is increasing, can damage authentic traditions.
Local festivals, which should be sacred, are often commercialized for profit.
On the other hand, travel helps us broaden our understanding of the world.
Eco-tourism, which is a viable alternative, protects pristine environments.
We should try to immerse ourselves respectfully.
If we do, the picturesque beauty remains out of this world.
To sum up, responsible travel is key.

questions.question_text: Do you prefer theoretical or practical learning? Does reading books cultivate your mind? (Recycled) How do teachers evaluate students in your country?

chunks.text: It is commonly believed that education is the key to success.
However, some systems focus too much on theoretical knowledge.
Actually, students often have to memorize facts without understanding them.
If schools had focused on cognitive skills earlier,
students would be better problem-solvers today.
We need a more comprehensive approach.
For example, we should cultivate creativity, not just memory.
If I hadn't had the chance to immerse myself in authentic projects,
I wouldn't be so confident now.
Unfortunately, many people learn the hard way that grades aren't everything.
We must put our thinking caps on to improve the system.

questions.question_text: Discuss whether schools should prioritize theory or practice.  The benefits of theory. The importance of practical skills. Your opinion on the best balance.

chunks.text: It is often debated whether schools should teach facts or skills.
Actually, both are important.
To be honest, pure theoretical learning can be boring.
If I hadn't been forced to memorize so many dates,
I would enjoy history more now.
However, we need to evaluate evidence critically.
If schools had cultivated more cognitive abilities in the past,
society would be smarter today.
I believe we need authentic tasks that force us to immerse in real work.
To sum up, a comprehensive education balances both.

questions.question_text: Is being an entrepreneur more lucrative than having a regular job? How can we cultivate good leadership skills? (Recycled) Do you like working in a strict hierarchy?

chunks.text: It is said that the workplace is changing.
In the past, there was a strict hierarchy.
Now, it is more flexible.
It is believed that being an entrepreneur is a lucrative path,
but it is risky.
To succeed, you must be versatile and able to delegate tasks.
Also, you need strong cognitive skills to solve problems.
It is expected that leaders cultivate a team that can think outside the box.
However, starting a business is hard work.
You often have to burn the midnight oil.
Ultimately, you want to fulfill your potential.

questions.question_text: Discuss how the corporate hierarchy is changing.  What traditional structures were like. How modern companies operate. The pros and cons of this change.

chunks.text: It is widely thought that traditional jobs are disappearing.
Actually, the hierarchy is becoming flatter.
To be honest, this allows employees to be more versatile.
It is reported that companies now value people who can
think outside the box and fulfill multiple roles.
Also, managers are learning to delegate more.
It is assumed that being an entrepreneur is the most lucrative option,
but it requires high cognitive resilience.
We must cultivate adaptability.
To sum up, work is about skills, not just titles.

questions.question_text: What are the consequences of a materialistic society? Is your current job lucrative enough for you? (Recycled) How do you allocate your monthly budget?

chunks.text: It is obvious that money matters.
However, there is a huge disparity between rich and poor.
Actually, we live in a very materialistic world.
Having observed society, I see that people buy things to compensate for sadness.
Ideally, wealth should be allocated more fairly.
Some jobs are indispensable but pay poorly.
Working hard, everyone should be able to flourish.
Unfortunately, even lucrative jobs are stressful.
Managers often fail to delegate work.
We should remember that money is just bread and butter, not everything.
Always save for a rainy day.

questions.question_text: Discuss the problem of wealth inequality.  Why there is a gap. How it affects people. What can be done.

chunks.text: It is undeniable that economic disparity is growing.
Actually, the rich get richer while the poor struggle.
To be honest, being materialistic doesn't make us happy.
Having realized this, many people are seeking a simpler life.
However, money is indispensable for survival.
Being a lucrative business, tech companies should compensate their workers better.
If we want society to flourish, we must allocate resources wisely.
Leaders need to delegate power to the community.
To sum up, fairness is key.

questions.question_text: Is the high prestige of professional athletes justified? Are sports indispensable for a society to flourish? (Recycled) Do you prefer amateur or professional sports?

chunks.text: It is shocking that athletes earn so much.
Rarely do we see such high salaries in other jobs.
Actually, brands pay them millions to endorse products.
Never have I seen an amateur player gain such prestige,
even if they work harder.
However, athletes must persevere through pain.
They exert enormous energy.
Little do people realize that team solidarity is indispensable for success.
If sports are to flourish, we need fairness.
Don't throw in the towel just because it's hard.
Always give it your best shot.

questions.question_text: Discuss the salaries of professional athletes.  Why they earn so much. If it is fair. The impact on society.

chunks.text: It is often debated whether athletes are overpaid.
Actually, their careers are short.
To be honest, rarely do people work as physically hard as they do.
Not only do they exert themselves daily,
but they also inspire solidarity among fans.
However, teachers are also indispensable but earn less.
Seldom have I seen a teacher flourish financially like a sports star.
Brands endorse athletes for their prestige.
Even an amateur must persevere.
To sum up, the market decides.

questions.question_text: How has digital communication affected human empathy? Should we endorse people who are superficial? (Recycled) How do you nurture a harmonious relationship?

chunks.text: It seems that technology connects us.
However, screens can alienate people.
Actually, online chats are often superficial.
What we need is more empathy towards others,
especially those who are vulnerable.
Some people hide behind avatars.
To build a harmonious society, we must nurture real connections.
It is trust that allows relationships to persevere through hard times.
I don't endorse cyberbullying.
True friends stick together through thick and thin.
We should always see the best in someone.

questions.question_text: Discuss the impact of screens on relationships.  How we communicate now. What is missing. How to improve empathy.

chunks.text: It is clear that digital communication is fast.
However, what worries me is the lack of empathy.
Actually, texts can be superficial and alienate friends.
It is face-to-face contact that helps us nurture harmonious bonds.
Without it, we feel vulnerable.
Although technology is useful, what matters most is that we persevere in meeting people.
I endorse putting phones away.
To sum up, real feelings need real presence.

questions.question_text: Do corporations have a moral duty to engage in philanthropy? How can we empower vulnerable groups? (Recycled) Is it truly possible to be 100% altruistic?

chunks.text: It is essential that society helps the poor.
However, many groups are still marginalized.
Actually, philanthropy is not just about giving money.
It is vital that big companies be more altruistic and support local initiatives.
I wish everyone were willing to do their bit.
Personally, I try to show empathy to those who are vulnerable.
If we were to mobilize our resources, we could empower many people.
It is time for leaders to step up to the plate.
We suggest that every student join a volunteer club.

questions.question_text: Discuss the role of charity in society.  Who should be responsible for helping. The benefits of volunteering. Challenges charities face.

chunks.text: It is often debated whether the government or individuals should do more.
Actually, philanthropy fills gaps that laws miss.
To be honest, I wish the government were more active in helping marginalized groups.
It is crucial that we empower vulnerable people rather than just giving them handouts.
Also, showing empathy is key.
If everyone were to mobilize for a common initiative, society would change.
We need altruistic leaders to step up to the plate.
To sum up, we must all do our bit.

questions.question_text: What role do resilience and tenacity play in overcoming obstacles? Does failing help you mobilize more effort next time? (Recycled) Have you ever achieved something unprecedented in your family?

chunks.text: It is widely recognized that success takes time.
Actually, having tenacious habits is key.
Many people strive to reach the pinnacle of their career.
Having faced many failures, they develop the resilience needed to continue.
However, talent is not enough.
You must be willing to go the extra mile.
I appreciate being empowered by my teachers to try new things.
Some athletes have achieved unprecedented records.
Actually, they surpass expectations against all odds.
In my opinion, mobilizeing your inner strength is vital.

questions.question_text: Discuss the importance of resilience.  What resilience means. A time you were resilient. Why it leads to success.

chunks.text: It is often said that resilience is more important than IQ.
Actually, life is full of challenges.
To be honest, having failed a math exam once, I felt terrible.
However, being supported by my parents helped me mobilize my energy to study again.
I decided to strive for better grades.
Having worked tenaciously for months, I eventually surpassed my classmates.
Reaching the pinnacle of the class was an unprecedented feeling for me.
I learned that you can succeed against all odds if
you go the extra mile.
To sum up, never give up.

questions.question_text: Is irreversible ecological damage inevitable? Can nature's resilience help it recover from unprecedented heat? (Recycled) How can we mitigate the effects of plastic waste?

chunks.text: It is undeniable that our planet is suffering.
Actually, we have caused unprecedented damage.
The more resources we use, the faster we deplete the earth.
The harder we try to grow the economy,
the more detrimental the ecological impact becomes.
Sadly, some damage is irreversible.
We risk wiping out vital biodiversity.
The longer we wait, the harder it will be to mitigate the disaster.
Although nature has resilience, it has limits.
My efforts feel like a drop in the ocean.
However, we must act now.

questions.question_text: Discuss the urgency of climate action.  What the main threats are. How our actions are linked to results. What must be done.

chunks.text: It is widely agreed that time is running out.
Actually, the loss of biodiversity is alarming.
To be honest, unprecedented storms are becoming common.
The more we deplete forests,
the less resilience the planet has against carbon.
We must mitigate these detrimental effects before they become irreversible.
The sooner we act, the better chance we have to avoid wiping out species.
Individual acts may seem like a drop in the ocean,
but together they matter.
To sum up, our ecological future is in our hands.

questions.question_text: How will workplace automation force society to adapt? Is the shift to AI an irreversible change? (Recycled) Do you think coding is an indispensable skill?

chunks.text: It is fascinating that tech evolves so fast.
Last year, our school was going to buy new computers.
However, they became obsolete very quickly.
They were going to automate the library system,
but the algorithm was too sophisticated.
Now, AI is a new paradigm.
Some people worry it will cause irreversible job losses.
We were going to mitigate this by training staff,
but the change is happening too fast.
Actually, this tech is light years ahead of old tools.
We shouldn't reinvent the wheel; we should use it.
Technology is now indispensable.

questions.question_text: Discuss how technology changes plans.  A plan you had involving tech. Why it changed. The outcome.

chunks.text: It is interesting that plans often fail due to tech speed.
Actually, my company was going to launch a simple app.
To be honest, we realized the code was obsolete.
We were going to automate customer service,
but we needed a more sophisticated algorithm.
This shift represents a new paradigm.
Although the delay was frustrating,
we managed to mitigate the loss and build something indispensable.
Our new AI is light years ahead.
To sum up, adaptation is key to avoid irreversible failure.

questions.question_text: Will globalization inevitably homogenize diverse cultures? Is the Western cultural paradigm too dominant? (Recycled) Do you want to assimilate into a new culture if you move abroad?

chunks.text: It is feared that globalization destroys diversity.
Some people think we will all become the same.
I hope not.
Actually, big brands tend to homogenize cities.
They sell sophisticated products that everyone wants,
but this creates a boring paradigm.
We must preserve our unique traditions.
If we don't, we lose our linguistic roots.
Immigrants often try to assimilate, but they shouldn't lose their identity.
Don't believe every stereotype you hear.
Some are true, but many aren't.
Our city is a melting pot.
Avoid culture shock by being open.

questions.question_text: Discuss the future of culture.  If cultures are becoming the same. Why this happens. How to keep traditions alive.

chunks.text: It is often asked if we are losing our identity.
I think so.
Actually, media tends to homogenize fashion and food.
To be honest, this paradigm endangers linguistic diversity.
People want to be sophisticated, so they copy global trends.
However, patriotism can help us preserve customs.
We welcome those who want to assimilate,
but we also value the old ways.
Living in a melting pot helps reduce stereotypes.
Will we lose everything?
I hope not.
To sum up, diversity matters.

questions.question_text: Does rapid urbanization create better amenitys or social alienation? Can we preserve nature in a sprawling city? (Recycled) Do you prefer to be anonymous in a big city?

chunks.text: It is true that cities offer jobs.
However, rapid urbanization causes problems.
Much as I love the convenience of every modern amenity,
the noise is stressful.
Also, cities can feel like a concrete jungle.
People often feel anonymous and lonely.
However hard we try to build communities, social alienation is increasing.
Furthermore, urban sprawl destroys nature.
It tends to displace local farmers.
Some developers homogenize neighborhoods, making them look the same.
We must try to preserve green spaces.
Escaping the rat race is becoming a priority for many.

questions.question_text: Discuss the pros and cons of city life.  Why people move to cities. The negative side effects. How to improve city living.

chunks.text: It is widely accepted that cities drive the economy.
Actually, urbanization brings better hospitals and schools.
To be honest, much as I enjoy the amenitys,
I hate the pollution.
In spite of living among millions,
it is easy to feel anonymous and suffer from alienation.
Also, the sprawl of housing threatens to displace wildlife.
However fast the city grows, we must preserve our parks.
Otherwise, we just live in a concrete jungle.
Modern life tends to homogenize our experiences.
To sum up, we need balance to avoid the rat race.

questions.question_text: How does consumerism affect our perception of status? Do impulsive purchases cause financial alienation? (Recycled) Why do celebrities endorse products?

chunks.text: It is worrying that we buy too much.
Actually, consumerism drives the economy but hurts the planet.
Companies manufacture goods cheaply.
Whatever you buy, it likely has a hidden environmental cost.
People often buy things to boost their status.
However, this is superficial happiness.
Whenever I feel impulsive, I try to wait before spending money.
Celebrities endorse luxury items that cost a fortune.
Trying to keep up with the Joneses leads to debt.
Also, shopping online keeps us anonymous but causes social alienation.
We should be smarter shoppers.

questions.question_text: Discuss the effects of advertising.  How ads influence us. If they are honest. Should they be regulated.

chunks.text: It is clear that ads are powerful.
Actually, they create impulsive desires.
To be honest, the link between products and status is superficial.
Whoever creates these ads knows how to manipulate our emotions.
They pay stars to endorse things we don't need.
Wherever you go, you see ads promoting consumerism.
Mass manufacture creates waste.
Buying things to keep up with the Joneses is foolish.
Even though online shopping is anonymous, it increases alienation.
To sum up, we must resist the pressure.

questions.question_text: Why is contemporary abstract art often controversial? Is judging art purely subjective or superficial? (Recycled) Does good art always evoke strong emotions?

chunks.text: It is often said that art is subjective.
Actually, abstract paintings confuse many people.
Understanding the artist's message requires a profound mind.
Some critics focus only on the aesthetic value.
However, contemporary art is about ideas.
The way colors are used can evoke deep sadness or joy.
Some say modern art is superficial and driven by consumerism.
But beauty is a matter of taste.
Great art sets the stage for new ways of thinking.
In my opinion, being subjective is what makes art interesting.

questions.question_text: Discuss the value of modern art.  Why it is popular. Why some dislike it. Your opinion.

chunks.text: It is undeniable that contemporary art divides opinion.
Actually, abstract pieces can look simple.
The reason why people pay millions is often linked to consumerism rather than talent.
However, art should evoke feelings.
Creating something unique sets a profound aesthetic standard.
Some critiques are superficial.
Whether you like it is a matter of taste.
I believe daring artists set the stage for future innovation.
To sum up, art is subjective.

questions.question_text: As longevity increases, how must society adapt to holistic care? Does a sedentary lifestyle evoke fear of chronic illness? (Recycled) Why are back problems so prevalent today?

chunks.text: It is alarming that we are living longer but sicker.
Actually, a sedentary lifestyle is so prevalent that everyone sits all day.
This habit takes a toll on our bodies.
Our health can deteriorate so fast that we develop chronic pain.
Doctors say we need a holistic approach.
We need to fix the mind and body.
It is such a profound problem that medicine alone isn't enough.
Even thinking about it can evoke worry.
However, with exercise, you can get back on your feet.
We aim for longevity with quality.

questions.question_text: Discuss the challenges of an aging population.  Why people live longer. The problems this creates. How we can help.

chunks.text: It is clear that longevity is increasing.
Actually, modern medicine is amazing.
However, chronic diseases are prevalent.
Some elderly people are so lonely that their mental health deteriorates.
Also, a sedentary life takes a toll on mobility.
This creates such a heavy burden on families that we
need a holistic care system.
Seeing them suffer evokes profound sadness.
We must help them get back on their feet emotionally.
To sum up, care is essential.

questions.question_text: Speculating on the next 50 years, what is humanity's greatest dilemma? Is it prevalent to fear that society will deteriorate? (Recycled) What optimum future do you anticipate?

chunks.text: It is exciting to speculate about 2070.
However, a climate crisis looms over us.
Actually, we face a serious dilemma.
Due to pollution, the environment is bound to deteriorate further unless we act.
Scientists anticipate major changes.
We are on the verge of a breakthrough that could
restore the optimum balance of nature.
We need visionary leaders.
Otherwise, suffering will become prevalent.
Will we succeed?
Only time will tell.
As for aliens, we will cross that bridge when we come to it.

questions.question_text: Discuss the future of humanity.  Biggest challenges. Technological hopes. Your prediction.

chunks.text: It is hard to know what lies ahead.
Actually, a resource war looms.
To be honest, this is our biggest dilemma.
Due to overpopulation, shortages are bound to become prevalent.
However, I anticipate that visionary inventors will save us.
We are on the verge of finding the optimum energy source.
If not, society will deteriorate.
Can we speculate on peace?
Only time will tell.
For now, we tackle today's problems and cross that bridge
when we come to it.
